l2_summary: The WHO has launched a platform to give free cancer treatments to children in low- and middle-income nations. Currently, the survival (7) ___ for children with cancer in poorer countries are under 30 percent, which is much lower than in (8) ___ nations. The project aims to expand to 50 countries in the next five to seven years, helping up to 120,000 (9) ___. Every year, approximately 400,000 children develop cancer worldwide, and many die due to poor-quality medicines or (10) ___ problems. This program was first announced in (11) ___ 2021 as a partnership between the WHO and St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, which provided 200 (12) ___ dollars to launch it. The program will offer (13) ___ medicines, and organizers are working to ensure its long-term (14) ___. According to Andre Ilbawi, childhood cancer is also becoming a major cause of (15) ___ in richer nations.
